conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.

<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of object X. My production cost was 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I need to sell X for more than 40 ZUP. Since this is the first proposal, I will start with a price that offers a reasonable profit margin but leaves room for negotiation. I will ask for 55 ZUP. This is 15 ZUP above my cost, which is a fair starting point. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 55 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have the object X you are looking for. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it to you for 55 ZUP. Let me know if you accept or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> Hello Player RED. 55 ZUP is a bit high for me. Since it cost you 40 ZUP to produce, I am willing to offer 48 ZUP. This is a fair price for both of us. Let me know if you accept. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 48 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 48 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP, so 48 ZUP would give me a profit of 8 ZUP. While this is a profit, I initially asked for 55 ZUP (15 ZUP profit). I have 2 proposals left, so I can try to meet in the middle. The midpoint between 48 and 55 is 51.5. Since I must use integers, I will propose 52 ZUP. This gives me a profit of 12 ZUP, which is better than 8, and shows I am willing to negotiate without dropping too low. If they reject this, I might have to accept 48 or reject if the offer is too low, but 52 seems like a strong middle ground. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 52 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand 55 was a bit high, and 48 leaves me with a very thin margin. How about we meet in the middle? I propose 52 ZUP. This gives me a decent profit and you get the object for less than my asking price. Is 52 ZUP acceptable? </message>

<other player message> I appreciate the move to 52 ZUP. However, 50 ZUP seems like a perfect middle ground between your cost of 40 and my budget. It's a round number and fair for both. If you agree to 50 ZUP, we can finalize the deal immediately. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 50 ZUP. This is a round number and offers me a profit of 10 ZUP (50 - 40). I have used 2 proposals so far. This is my 3rd and final proposal opportunity. If I make a counter-proposal now, I will have used all my proposal slots, and if they reject my next offer, I will be forced to either Accept or Reject their current offer of 50 ZUP. Given that 50 ZUP is a fair profit (25% return on cost) and they emphasized "finalizing the deal immediately," pushing for a higher price (like 51 or 52) might risk them walking away or forcing me into a bad position after my proposals run out. It is strategically better to Accept this offer now to secure the profit rather than risk losing the deal or being forced to accept a lower price later. However, the rules say I can propose up to 3 times. I have made 2 proposals. I can make one more. But is it worth it? If I propose 51, they might say no, and then I have to Accept 50 or Reject. If I Accept 50 now, I get 10 ZUP profit. If I propose 51 and they say no, I still likely end up accepting 50. So proposing 51 is a waste of a turn unless I think they will accept 51. They said 50 is "perfect". I will Accept 50 ZUP to close the deal safely. Wait, the instructions say "You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own". I have made 2. I can make a 3rd. But if I make a 3rd proposal and they reject, I must Accept or Reject their *last* offer (which was 50). If I Accept now, the game ends. If I propose 51, and they reject, I have to Accept 50. So there is no downside to Accepting 50 now, and a potential upside (closing the game faster). Actually, if I propose 51 and they accept, I get 11 profit. If they reject, I get 10 profit (by accepting 50). Is the risk of them walking away (Rejecting the game entirely) worth it? They seem reasonable. But 50 is a solid offer. I will Accept 50 ZUP to ensure the trade happens. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> That sounds fair. 50 ZUP is a good middle ground. I accept your offer of 50 ZUP for object X. Deal is done! </message>
